title: Koeleria mendocinensis
taxonomy: Koeleria mendocinensis belongs to the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. It is a type of perennial grass native to California. The genus Koeleria is a group of grasses that are typically found in dry to moist environments. Within the genus, Koeleria mendocinensis is a specific species that has been identified and classified. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Koeleria mendocinensis.

The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer.
